---
title: "3. Commercial Intelligence Specification"
subtitle: "The governing method, in brief"
---

::: {.chapter-chips}
<span class="chip chip-neutral no-dot">Working Paper · applied discussion</span>
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" icon=false}
**This chapter vs. the standard.** This is the *applied discussion* — how the CIS method is used in
this project. For the **normative standard itself** (lifecycle, ontology, evidence model, mandatory
rules), see [Governance › Commercial Intelligence Specification](../cis/commercial-intelligence-specification.md).
:::

::: {.wp-thesis}
::: {.wp-t-cell}
::: {.wp-t-label}
Question
:::
How do we ensure every commercial recommendation in this project is traceable, reproducible,
falsifiable, and honest about its confidence?
:::
::: {.wp-t-cell}
::: {.wp-t-label}
Hypothesis
:::
Treating commercial strategy as a **computational decision system** — with a fixed lifecycle, an
evidence model, and an enforced testing standard — produces better, more revisable decisions than
narrative strategy documents.
:::
:::

### The method in brief

::: {.wp-fields}
::: {.wp-field}
### Lifecycle
Question → Hypothesis → Operational Definition → Evidence → Analysis → Model → Validation →
Recommendation → Implementation → Monitoring → Learning.
:::
::: {.wp-field}
### Ontology & object model
Every claim, score, and decision is a typed object with identity and provenance
([`ontology.yml`](../cis/ontology.yml), [`object-model.yml`](../cis/object-model.yml)).
:::
::: {.wp-field}
### Evidence model
Items are weighted by quality × bias adjustment; each claim gets a strength grade.
:::
::: {.wp-field}
### Confidence grades
D (hypothesis) → C (indicative) → B (substantiated) → A (validated). All logic under `pytest`.
:::
:::

::: {.wp-fields}
::: {.wp-field}
### Current evidence
- The specification, ontology, object model, and lifecycle YAML exist and are internally consistent.
- The test suite passes (see [Ch. 8](08-validation-and-tests.qmd)).
:::
::: {.wp-field}
### Evidence needed
- Evidence that the method is actually followed (provenance present on recommendations).
- A green test suite as a precondition for trusting any number.
:::
::: {.wp-field}
### Analysis plan
Audit each downstream chapter for lifecycle compliance: does each recommendation-in-waiting name its
evidence and validation? Flag any number presented above its earned confidence grade.
:::
::: {.wp-field}
### Next steps
Apply the method to the three decision questions (Chs. 4–7).
:::
:::

::: {.wp-field .is-caution .is-full}
### Limitations
- v0.1 enforces *structure*, not advanced modelling; sophistication is deferred (CIS §21).
:::
